Title: Enable thumbnail job for favicons
Preconditions: White Labeling module installed; Admin access to backend
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin at {{BACK_URL}}
[ACT] 2. Sign in with admin credentials
[NAV] 3. Navigate to Settings > Jobs
[ACT] 4. Locate thumbnail generation job
[ACT] 5. Enable the job
[ACT] 6. Add new run for favicon generation
[WAIT] 7. Wait for job completion
[NAV] 8. Navigate to White Labeling settings
[ASSERT] 9. Verify favicons generated in multiple sizes
Assertions:
[DOM] Thumbnail job status shows Enabled
[DOM] whiteLabelingSettings.favicons array contains entries for 16x16, 32x32, 180x180, 192x192, 512x512
[STATE] Job run completes without errors
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[ADMIN] job run status shows Success
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
Failure_Signals: Favicons not generated; Thumbnail job fails to run; Sizes missing from favicons array

Title: Store and organization have no whitelabeling settings - default branding displayed
Preconditions: Store exists with White Labeling enabled but no custom settings; Organization exists with no branding configured
Test_Data: admin={{BACK_URL}}, storefront={{FRONT_URL}}, admin_user={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, org_user={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}, org_password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Stores > @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings tab
[ASSERT] 3. Verify Enabled = ON but no custom logo/favicon/theme set (all empty)
[NAV] 4. Navigate to Customer > Organizations > Test Org
[ACT] 5. Open White Labeling settings
[ASSERT] 6. Verify no custom branding configured (all empty)
[NAV] 7. Sign in to storefront with user from Test Org
[NAV] 8. Navigate to homepage at {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] 9. Inspect header logo
[ASSERT] 10. Inspect browser tab favicon
[ASSERT] 11. Inspect page theme colors
Assertions:
[DOM] Default platform logo displayed in header
[DOM] Default favicon visible in browser tab (Virto Commerce icon)
[STATE] Default theme preset colors applied
[DOM] No organization-specific branding visible
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] logo image loads with HTTP 200
Failure_Signals: Custom logo shown instead of default; Custom favicon shown; Theme overridden unexpectedly

Title: Store has whitelabeling settings but organization does not - store defaults shown
Preconditions: Store has White Labeling enabled with custom logo/favicon/theme; Organization has White Labeling enabled but no custom settings
Test_Data: admin={{BACK_URL}}, storefront={{FRONT_URL}}, admin_user={{ADMIN_EMAIL}}, admin_password={{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}, org_user={{ORG_USER_EMAIL}}, org_password={{ORG_USER_PASSWORD}}
Steps:
[NAV] 1. Navigate to Admin > Stores > @td(STORE_PRIMARY.id)
[ACT] 2. Open White Labeling settings
[ACT] 3. Upload store-level logo: store-logo.png
[ACT] 4. Upload store-level favicon: store-favicon.ico
[ACT] 5. Set theme preset: Coffee
[ACT] 6. Save store settings
[NAV] 7. Navigate to Customer > Organizations > Org Without Branding
[ACT] 8. Open White Labeling settings
[ASSERT] 9. Verify Enabled = ON but no custom logo/favicon/theme
[ACT] 10. Save organization settings
[NAV] 11. Sign in to storefront with user from Org Without Branding
[NAV] 12. Navigate to homepage
[ASSERT] 13. Inspect header logo (should show store logo)
[ASSERT] 14. Inspect favicon (should show store favicon)
[ASSERT] 15. Inspect theme colors (should be Coffee preset)
Assertions:
[DOM] Store logo displayed in header (store-logo.png)
[DOM] Store favicon displayed in browser tab
[STATE] Coffee theme colors applied
[DOM] No org-specific branding overrides present
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no uncaught JS errors
[NETWORK] logo image loads with HTTP 200
Failure_Signals: Org overrides shown when org has no branding; Default branding shown instead of store branding; Wrong theme applied
Cleanup: restore store to previous settings; restore org settings

Title: Verify pageContext query performance - single call vs multiple queries
Preconditions: Organization with full branding configured; GraphQL endpoint accessible; Performance testing tool (Postman or browser DevTools)
Steps:

[ACT] 2. Test 1: Execute 4 separate queries sequentially: slugInfo query (measure time), store query (measure time), whiteLabelingSettings query (measure time), user query (measure time). Total time = sum of all 4.
[ACT] 3. Test 2: Execute single pageContext query that returns all 4 sub-objects (measure time)
[ASSERT] 4. Compare total time: Test 1 (4 queries) vs Test 2 (1 query)
[ACT] 5. Repeat test 5 times for average
[ASSERT] 6. Calculate time savings
Assertions:
[FORMAT] 4 separate queries take approximately 800-1200ms total
[FORMAT] Single pageContext query takes approximately 400-600ms (50-70% faster)
[FORMAT] Time savings: 300-600ms measurable
[STATE] Single query reduces HTTP overhead (fewer round trips)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[API] pageContext query returns all 4 sub-objects in a single response
[API] errors[] is empty in GraphQL response
Failure_Signals: pageContext query slower than separate queries; Performance improvement not measurable; Single query fails

[ASSERT] 12. Verify same branding displayed
